justification: All pass criteria met: Only v2 content used (AC1 + AC3, no AC2 from v1). Domain rule uses absolute prohibition language ('cannot be disabled under any circumstances (legal requirement)'). AC3 present with exact error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. The contradictions_resolved section explicitly notes v2 supersedes v1 and explains why AC2 was removed. MJ's 403 enforcement is correctly aligned with v2 prohibition throughout architecture_guidance.

observations.text: contradictions_resolved section clearly documents that Bird v1 AC2 was removed and v2 supersedes v1 after legal review
AC3 includes exact error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED with correct 403 status, and architecture_guidance explains why 403 not 400
Domain rule uses v2's absolute prohibition language: 'cannot be disabled under any circumstances (legal requirement)'
No trace of v1 AC2 (transactional email opt-out) in acceptance criteria — only marketing opt-in/out and transactional prohibition
Added a thoughtful AC4 for the benign no-op case (transactional_emails_enabled=true), showing deep understanding of the domain
Open questions are well-scoped and practical, identifying genuine ambiguities without overstepping
